dashboard.title: Questorr Configuration
dashboard.subtitle: Questorr needs to be connected with your Discord bot and with your Jellyfin and Seerr instances.
dashboard.subtitle_detail: Below is a step-by-step walkthrough to help you set up everything you need to get started!
dashboard.login_subtitle: Secure, self-hosted bridge for your media server.

config.title: Configuration
config.stats_reset_confirm: Reset all statistics? This cannot be undone.
config.language_help: Select the language for the Questorr dashboard.
config.discord_instructions_intro: Create a Discord application for the bot. Access the Discord Developer Portal and log in. Create a new application, then:
config.discord_instructions_1: Go to "Bot" in the sidebar and click "Add Bot"
config.discord_instructions_2: In the "Bot" section, scroll down to "Privileged Gateway Intents" and enable SERVER MEMBERS INTENT (required for user mappings)
config.discord_instructions_6: Copy the generated URL, open it in your browser, and authorize the bot on your Discord server
config.discord_instructions_7: Come back and enter the Discord Bot Token and Bot Client ID below, save your settings, start the bot, then select your server and channel from the dropdowns
config.discord_token_help: The secret token of your bot from the Discord Developer Portal → Bot → Reset Token
config.bot_id_help: The application client ID from the Discord Developer Portal → OAuth2 → Client ID
config.next_step_1: Click the Save Settings button at the bottom of the sidebar
config.next_step_2: Click the Start Bot button at the top of the page
config.next_step_3: Once the bot is running, the dropdowns will automatically populate with your servers and channels
config.auto_start_info: Auto-Start: The bot will automatically start when you enter and save Discord credentials for the first time.
config.discord_server_help: Select the Discord server where the bot will operate. Make sure you have added the bot to your server first.
config.episode_channel_help: Optional: Choose a different channel specifically for episode notifications. Leave empty to use the default channel.
config.season_channel_help: Optional: Choose a different channel specifically for season notifications. Leave empty to use the default channel.
config.default_channel_help: Default Discord channel for notifications. Can be overridden per library in Step 4.
config.seerr_url_help: The full URL of your Seerr instance.
config.seerr_api_key_help: Your API key from Seerr settings
config.default_quality_profiles_help: Set default quality profiles and servers for media requests.
config.default_tv_quality_help: Quality profile for TV show requests (Sonarr)
config.default_tv_server_help: Sonarr server for TV show requests
config.tmdb_api_key_help: Required for searching and fetching details. Found in the API settings page of your TMDB account.
config.omdb_api_key_help: Optional, but recommended for richer data. Create one on the API Key page of OMDb.
config.jellyfin_url_help: The public URL of your Jellyfin server. Used for the "Watch Now" button.
config.jellyfin_api_key_help: API key for your Jellyfin server. You can generate one in Jellyfin Dashboard → Administration → API Keys. This is required for loading libraries and enhanced features.
config.test_endpoint_help: Testing the endpoint successfully will automatically populate the Jellyfin Server ID field.
config.jellyfin_server_id_help: Used for redirecting to the correct path of your Jellyfin server media page when using the "Watch Now" button. You can find this in your Jellyfin URL when viewing the page of any movie or show from your library.
config.notification_testing_help: Test your notification templates by sending sample notifications to your Discord channel. This helps you preview how different types of notifications will appear.
config.seerr_channels_desc: Configure which Discord channels receive Seerr event notifications.
config.seerr_default_channel_desc: Receives: Approved, Available, Declined, Failed, Issues
config.seerr_root_folder_desc: Route notifications to the correct Discord channel based on Radarr/Sonarr root folder.
config.request_ui_desc: Choose which optional fields are visible when users run /request.
config.show_tag_selection_desc: Users can select a Radarr/Sonarr tag (e.g. 'anime')
config.show_server_selection_desc: Users can choose between multiple Radarr/Sonarr instances
config.show_quality_selection_desc: Users can select a quality profile for the download
config.show_status_command_desc: Check the Seerr request status of any title
config.show_random_command_desc: Pick a random movie or series from Jellyfin
config.daily_recommendation_desc: Send a daily recommendation from your existing Jellyfin library to a Discord channel.
config.seerr_webhook_box_desc: Go to Seerr → Settings → Notifications → Webhook and enter this URL:
config.seerr_webhook_replace: Replace localhost with your server IP if using Docker.
config.seerr_webhook_header_desc: In Seerr, also add an authorization header to authenticate the webhook:
config.jellyfin_fallback_desc: The fallback channel is configured in tab 1. Discord → Default Notification Channel.
config.jellyfin_note_text: Questorr uses the Jellyfin API internally to determine the correct Discord channel for Seerr notifications.
config.jellyfin_note_text2: The Library Channel Mapping is configured via tab 2. Seerr → Seerr Notification Channels.
config.jellyfin_retry_help: When a new item is available, Jellyfin may not have scanned it yet. Questorr will retry the library lookup after this delay (up to 3 times). Set to 0 to disable. Max: 600s (10 min).
config.jellyfin_autofill: Automatically filled in when you click the Test button.
config.mappings_desc: Map Discord users to their Seerr accounts. Requests from Discord will be associated with the corresponding Seerr account.
config.roles_desc: Control who can use bot commands. If no roles are selected in the allowlist, all members can use commands.
config.roles_allowlist_desc: If empty, all members can use commands. If roles are selected, only members with these roles can use commands.
config.roles_blocklist_desc: Members with these roles cannot use bot commands, even if they have an allowed role.
config.misc_desc: Optional settings to enhance your bot experience.
config.misc_autostart_desc: If enabled, the bot starts automatically when the container restarts and Discord credentials are present.
config.misc_pm: Send DM when request is available
config.misc_pm_desc: When enabled, users will receive a DM when their requested content becomes available on Jellyfin.
config.misc_ephemeral_desc: When enabled, all bot responses are only visible to the user. When disabled, successful results are visible to everyone.
config.misc_autoapprove_desc: When enabled, requests through the bot are automatically approved in Seerr. If disabled, requests remain 'Pending'.
config.widget_desc: Provide an embeddable HTML widget for Homarr, Homepage or Organizr. Shows bot status, uptime, command statistics and start/stop controls.
config.widget_api_key_desc: Required. This key protects the widget endpoints from unauthorized access.
config.widget_url_desc: Use this URL as an iFrame source in Homarr, Homepage or Organizr:
config.widget_url_note: The API key is included in the URL but hidden from the display above. Save your config first to apply changes.
config.widget_allowed_origins_help: Space-separated list of origins allowed to embed the widget in an iFrame. Leave empty for same-origin only. Use * to allow all origins.
config.widget_anonymize_help: When enabled, usernames and avatars in widget stats are replaced with "User 1", "User 2", etc. to protect privacy.
config.rate_limit_desc: Maximum Discord commands per user per minute. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.
config.embed_footer_desc: Custom footer text shown on all Discord embeds. Leave empty for no footer.
config.embed_extra_help: Leave country fields empty to auto-detect from bot language (e.g. DE for German, US for English).
config.media_type_channels_desc: Route Seerr notifications to different channels based on media type. Takes priority over the default Seerr channel but is overridden by root folder mappings.
config.request_ui_warning: ⚠️ After saving, Discord commands will be updated automatically. Then press Ctrl+R in Discord (desktop) or reload the page (browser) to see the changes.
config.daily_random_desc: Send a daily random media recommendation to a Discord channel. Great for discovering hidden gems!
config.daily_random_interval_help: How often to send a pick in minutes (default: 1440 = 24 hours)
config.daily_rec_desc: Send a daily recommendation from your existing Jellyfin library to a Discord channel.
config.daily_rec_interval_help: How often to send a recommendation in minutes (default: 1440 = 24 hours)
config.embed_colors_desc: Customize the colors of Discord notification embeds.
config.seerr_root_folder_hint: ⚠️ Click "Load Root Folders" first before adding a mapping.
config.cmd_options_desc: Configure which options are shown or available for each Discord command.
config.status_cmd_desc: Always available — no configuration required. Users can check the current request status of any title in Seerr directly in Discord.
config.notification_buttons_desc: Default buttons for all notifications. Use the table below to override per event type.
config.btn_view_seerr_desc: Link to the media page in Seerr
config.btn_watch_now_desc: Direct link to the Jellyfin player (available content only)
config.btn_letterboxd_desc: Link to the Letterboxd page (movies only)
config.command_toggles_desc: Enable or disable individual Discord slash commands.
config.notif_titles_desc: Customize the title line of each Discord notification. Leave empty to use the default for the selected Bot Language.
config.bot_language_help: Language for Discord notifications, bot messages and button labels.
config.export_import_desc: Download your current configuration as a backup, or restore it from a previously exported file. API keys are masked in the export — they will not be overwritten on import.
config.notif_buttons_per_event_desc: Override the default buttons per event type. Pre-filled from Default Buttons above.
config.notif_btn_col_hint: — = use global setting | ✅ = always show | ☐ = always hide
config.seerr_webhook_disabled: Webhook notifications are disabled in Seerr.
config.seerr_notif_type_disabled: This notification type is not enabled in Seerr.
config.notif_buttons_reset_hint: Resets all rows to the Standard-Buttons above.
config.seerr_root_folder_optional: 💡 Optional: If you only have one root folder per media type, you can skip this and use the Jellyfin Library → Channel mapping in Step 4 instead.
config.jellyfin_load_key_missing: Please enter Jellyfin API Key first.
config.roles_blocklist_info: If any role is added to the blocklist, members with that role will be blocked regardless of allowlist settings.
config.daily_random_channel_help: Discord channel for daily random picks
config.daily_rec_channel_help: Discord channel for daily recommendations from your Jellyfin library

search.description: Search for a movie/TV show (you can request it later)
search.error: An error occurred while processing your request.

request.description: Instantly send a request for a movie/TV show

trending.description: Browse trending movies and TV shows

errors.loading_members_bot_running: Error loading members. Is bot running?
errors.bot_must_be_running: Bot must be running to load roles
errors.mapping_select_both: Please select both a Discord user and a Seerr user.
errors.refresh_users_failed: Failed to refresh users. Check connections.
errors.jellyfin_load_libraries: Failed to load libraries. Please check your Jellyfin URL and API Key.
errors.seerr_enter_url_key: Enter URL and API Key first

about.title: About Questorr
about.description: Questorr is a self-hosted bridge between your Jellyfin media server and Discord. It connects Jellyfin and Seerr so your community can search, request and track media — all without leaving Discord.
about.feature_search:
/search <title> Search for movies and TV shows with auto-suggestions. A rich embed with poster, description, rating and genre is shown — request directly from the embed. For TV shows, you can select individual seasons.
Options: title (required) — the movie or show title to search for. Auto-complete suggests matching titles as you type.
about.feature_request:
/request <title> Instantly send a media request to Seerr. For TV shows, all seasons are requested automatically.
Options: title (required) — the title to request. tag (optional) — select a tag (e.g. anime). server (optional) — choose a Radarr/Sonarr server. quality (optional) — select a quality profile. Optional fields are only visible if enabled in the dashboard under Step 2.
about.feature_trending:
/trending Browse the week's most popular movies and TV shows from TMDB — discover what's hot right now and request directly.
Options: title (required) — select from the trending list via auto-complete.
about.feature_notifications: Receive rich Discord notifications for every Seerr event: pending approval, approved, available, declined, failed and issues — each routed to the right channel automatically.
about.feature_routing: Automatically route MEDIA_AVAILABLE notifications to the correct Discord channel based on the Radarr/Sonarr root folder — one channel per library category.
about.feature_databases: Embeds are enriched with data from TMDB and OMDb: posters, backdrops, IMDb ratings, runtime, genre and a direct Watch Now link.
about.feature_permissions: Control who can use bot commands via Discord role allowlist and blocklist. Link Discord accounts to Seerr accounts so every request is tracked to the correct user.
about.feature_direct_messages: Users receive a private DM when their requested content becomes available on Jellyfin — so nobody misses the moment their request is ready.
about.feature_daily: Post a Daily Random Pick from TMDB or a Daily Recommendation from your existing Jellyfin library into a Discord channel — great for community engagement.
about.feature_dashboard: Configure everything through the web dashboard at port 8282. Supports English and German, is fully mobile-responsive and works on smartphones and tablets.
about.support_description: If you find Questorr useful, consider supporting its development. Every coffee helps during late-night coding sessions!
about.contribute_description: Questorr is open-source under the AGPL-3.0 license. Contributions, bug reports and feature requests are very welcome — check out the project on GitHub.
about.discord_description: Have questions or want to chat with other Questorr users? Join the Jellyforge Discord server.
about.footer_thanks: Thank you for using Questorr! Feel free to open a
about.feature_status:
/status <title> Check the current Seerr request status of any title in Discord — with poster, summary, genre, runtime, rating and age rating. Shows a Request button if not yet requested.
Options: title (required) — the title to check. Auto-complete suggests matching titles.
about.feature_random:
/random movie or /random series — get a random suggestion from your Jellyfin library with poster, summary, genre, runtime, rating and Watch Now button.
Options: type (required) — choose movie or series.
about.feature_button_toggles: Configure which notification buttons (View on Seerr, Watch Now, Letterboxd, IMDb) appear on Discord embeds — individually togglable in the dashboard.
about.feature_media_type_routing: Route notifications to different Discord channels based on media type (movies vs series). Configurable alongside root folder and library-based routing.
about.feature_health_check: Built-in health-check endpoint at /api/health for Docker HEALTHCHECK and monitoring tools like Uptime Kuma. Reports bot status, service connectivity and cache stats.
about.feature_widget: Embeddable HTML widget for Homarr, Homepage or Organizr. Shows bot status, uptime, command statistics with per-user breakdown, and start/stop controls — secured by API key.
about.feature_rate_limiting: Per-user command rate limiting prevents Discord API abuse. Configurable commands-per-minute threshold per user.
about.feature_watchlist:
/watchlist View recent Seerr requests directly in Discord. Paginated with ◀/▶ buttons for easy browsing.
Options: filter (optional) — all = all requests, mine = only your own requests (requires user mapping), pending = requests awaiting approval, available = already available content. Default: all.
about.feature_upcoming:
/upcoming Browse upcoming releases from TMDB — movies and TV shows that haven't aired yet. Only future releases are shown.
Options: type (optional) — all = movies and TV shows, movie = movies only, tv = TV shows only. Default: all.
about.feature_history:
/history See what was recently added to your Jellyfin library. Great for catching up on new content.
Options: type (optional) — all = everything, movie = movies only, series = series only. Default: all.
about.feature_recommend: /recommend <title> Get personalized recommendations based on any movie or TV show. Shows 5 similar titles with TMDB ratings and Jellyfin availability status — with Watch Now buttons for content already in your library.
about.feature_approve_decline: Admins can approve or decline pending Seerr requests directly from the Discord notification — no need to open the Seerr dashboard.
about.feature_embed_footer: Add a custom footer text to all Discord embeds — perfect for branding, server info or a personal touch.
about.feature_config_backup: Export and import your entire configuration as JSON. API keys are masked in exports for security — ideal for backups or migrating to a new server.
about.feature_discover:
/discover Explore movies and TV shows by genre, year and minimum rating. Results show Jellyfin availability — perfect for "I want to watch something but don't know what" moments.
Options: type (required), genre (optional, autocomplete), year (optional), rating (optional).
about.feature_collection: /collection <title> View all movies in a franchise or collection (e.g. Lord of the Rings, Marvel). Shows which parts are available on Jellyfin with Watch Now buttons.
about.feature_cast: /cast <name> Browse an actor's filmography with character names, ratings and Jellyfin availability. Sorted by popularity — autocomplete suggests actors with their known roles.
about.feature_similar: /similar <title> Find similar titles based on genre and keywords — a different algorithm than /recommend. Shows availability status and Watch Now buttons.
about.feature_content_rating: Content ratings (FSK, MPAA) are shown in search embeds — configurable by country. Know if a title is age-appropriate before requesting.
about.feature_providers: Streaming providers show where a title is available for streaming (Netflix, Disney+, etc.) — powered by TMDB and JustWatch data.
about.feature_trailer: Trailer buttons are automatically added to /search and /request embeds when a YouTube trailer is available on TMDB — watch the trailer before requesting.

jellyfin_webhook.note: If you're running Questorr in Docker, you may need to replace localhost with your server's IP address.
jellyfin_webhook.plugin_install: If you don't have the plugin installed, access the Plugins page in your Jellyfin Dashboard, search for Webhook, install it and restart the server.
jellyfin_webhook.plugin_setup: After the restart, access your Webhook plugin settings and Add Generic Destination.
jellyfin_webhook.step_server_url: Complete the Server Url with your Jellyfin server's base URL.
jellyfin_webhook.step_webhook_url: Insert the previously specified URL in the Webhook Url field.
jellyfin_webhook.step_notification_type: For Notification Type enable Item Added.
jellyfin_webhook.step_item_type: For Item Type select your desired content you want to receive notifications for.
jellyfin_webhook.step_headers: Scroll down to the Headers section and click Add Header. Set the name to X-Webhook-Secret and paste your copied secret as the value.
jellyfin_webhook.generic_destination_notice: Jellyfin does not support custom headers for the Discord Destination type. If you previously used a Discord Destination, you need to delete it and recreate it as a Generic Destination — otherwise the X-Webhook-Secret header cannot be added and Questorr will reject all incoming requests.
jellyfin_webhook.webhook_secret_desc: Your webhook secret is used to secure incoming requests. Copy it below — you'll need to add it as a custom HTTP header in the Jellyfin webhook plugin:
jellyfin_webhook.notification_testing_info: These tests bypass the webhook endpoint and run internally — they work without the X-Webhook-Secret header. Make sure you've still copied the secret and added it to your Jellyfin webhook plugin so that real notifications from Jellyfin are accepted.
